 `./pkg/utils/project_identity.go`

```text
1	package utils
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/sha256"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"log/slog"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"regexp"
11		"strings"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/google/uuid"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
16	)
17	
18	const PROJECT_JSON_FILE = ".project.json"
19	
20	// ProjectIdentity represents the project identity stored in .specstory/.project.json
21	type ProjectIdentity struct {
22		WorkspaceID   string `json:"workspace_id"`
23		WorkspaceIDAt string `json:"workspace_id_at"`
24		GitID         string `json:"git_id,omitempty"`
25		GitIDAt       string `json:"git_id_at,omitempty"`
26		ProjectName   string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
27	}
28	
29	// ProjectIdentityManager handles project identity operations
30	type ProjectIdentityManager struct {
31		projectRoot string
32	}
33	
34	// NewProjectIdentityManager creates a new project identity manager
35	func NewProjectIdentityManager(projectRoot string) *ProjectIdentityManager {
36		return &ProjectIdentityManager{
37			projectRoot: projectRoot,
38		}
39	}
40	
41	// getProjectJSONPath returns the path to .specstory/.project.json
42	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) getProjectJSONPath() string {
43		return filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, SPECSTORY_DIR, PROJECT_JSON_FILE)
44	}
45	
46	// EnsureProjectIdentity initializes or updates the project identity
47	// Returns true if the identity was created or modified
48	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) EnsureProjectIdentity() (bool, error) {
49		slog.Debug("Ensuring project identity", "projectRoot", m.projectRoot)
50	
51		// Ensure .specstory directory exists (create if needed)
52		specstoryDir := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, SPECSTORY_DIR)
53		if err := os.MkdirAll(specstoryDir, 0755); err != nil {
54			return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to create .specstory directory: %w", err)
55		}
56	
57		// Read existing project identity if it exists
58		projectJSONPath := m.getProjectJSONPath()
59		existingIdentity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
60		if err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
61			return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to read existing project identity: %w", err)
62		}
63	
64		// Determine what needs to be done
65		var identity ProjectIdentity
66		isModified := false
67	
68		isNewProject := false
69		if existingIdentity == nil {
70			// Case 1: No .project.json yet
71			slog.Debug("No existing project identity found, creating new identity")
72			identity = ProjectIdentity{
73				WorkspaceID:   m.generateWorkspaceID(),
74				WorkspaceIDAt: time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
75			}
76			isModified = true
77			isNewProject = true
78		} else {
79			// Case 2 or 3: .project.json exists
80			identity = *existingIdentity
81	
82			// Check if workspace_id is missing (shouldn't happen, but be defensive)
83			if identity.WorkspaceID == "" {
84				slog.Warn("Project identity file exists but has no workspace_id")
85				identity.WorkspaceID = m.generateWorkspaceID()
86				identity.WorkspaceIDAt = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
87				isModified = true
88			}
89		}
90	
91		// Check if we need to add git_id
92		if identity.GitID == "" {
93			gitID, err := m.generateGitID()
94			if err == nil && gitID != "" {
95				identity.GitID = gitID
96				identity.GitIDAt = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
97				isModified = true
98				slog.Debug("Added git_id to project identity", "git_id", gitID)
99			} else if err != nil {
100				slog.Debug("Could not generate git_id", "error", err)
101			}
102		}
103	
104		// Check if we need to add project_name
105		if identity.ProjectName == "" {
106			projectName := m.generateProjectName()
107			if projectName != "" {
108				identity.ProjectName = projectName
109				isModified = true
110				slog.Debug("Added project_name to project identity", "project_name", projectName)
111			} else {
112				slog.Warn("Could not generate project_name, using workspace_id instead")
113				identity.ProjectName = identity.WorkspaceID
114			}
115		}
116	
117		// Write the project identity file if modified
118		if isModified {
119			jsonData, err := json.MarshalIndent(identity, "", "  ")
120			if err != nil {
121				return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal project identity: %w", err)
122			}
123	
124			if err := os.WriteFile(projectJSONPath, jsonData, 0644); err != nil {
125				return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to write project identity: %w", err)
126			}
127	
128			slog.Info("Project identity saved", "path", projectJSONPath, "identity", identity)
129	
130			// Track analytics for new project creation
131			if isNewProject {
132				properties := analytics.Properties{
133					"has_git_id":       identity.GitID != "",
134					"has_workspace_id": identity.WorkspaceID != "",
135					"project_name":     identity.ProjectName,
136				}
137	
138				// Determine the ID type being used
139				if identity.GitID != "" {
140					properties["id_type"] = "git"
141				} else {
142					properties["id_type"] = "workspace"
143				}
144	
145				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventProjectIdentityCreated, properties)
146			}
147	
148			return true, nil
149		}
150	
151		slog.Debug("Project identity is up-to-date, no changes written")
152		return false, nil
153	}
154	
155	// ReadProjectIdentity reads the project identity from .project.json
156	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) ReadProjectIdentity() (*ProjectIdentity, error) {
157		projectJSONPath := m.getProjectJSONPath()
158	
159		data, err := os.ReadFile(projectJSONPath)
160		if err != nil {
161			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
162				return nil, err
163			}
164			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read project identity: %w", err)
165		}
166	
167		// Remove trailing commas before closing braces/brackets to handle malformed JSON
168		// This regex finds commas followed by optional whitespace and a closing brace/bracket
169		cleanedData := regexp.MustCompile(`,\s*([}\]])`).ReplaceAll(data, []byte("$1"))
170	
171		var identity ProjectIdentity
172		if err := json.Unmarshal(cleanedData, &identity); err != nil {
173			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal project identity: %w", err)
174		}
175	
176		return &identity, nil
177	}
178	
179	// generateWorkspaceID generates a workspace ID by hashing the full project path
180	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateWorkspaceID() string {
181		// Get absolute path to ensure consistency
182		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(m.projectRoot)
183		if err != nil {
184			slog.Warn("Failed to get absolute path, using relative path", "error", err)
185			absPath = m.projectRoot
186		}
187	
188		// The TypeScript version hashes the workspace URI, but in our case
189		// we'll hash the absolute path for compatibility
190		return m.createHash(absPath)
191	}
192	
193	// generateGitID generates a git ID by finding and hashing the git origin URL
194	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateGitID() (string, error) {
195		gitConfigPath := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, ".git", "config")
196	
197		// Check if git config exists
198		if _, err := os.Stat(gitConfigPath); err != nil {
199			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git config found: %w", err)
200		}
201	
202		// Read git config file
203		gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
204		if err != nil {
205			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read git config: %w", err)
206		}
207	
208		// Find origin remote URL using regex
209		// Match [remote "origin"] section and capture the url value
210		originURLRegex := regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
211		matches := originURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
212		if len(matches) < 2 {
213			return "", fmt.Errorf("no origin URL found in git config")
214		}
215	
216		originURL := strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
217		if originURL == "" {
218			return "", fmt.Errorf("no URL found for origin remote")
219		}
220	
221		// Normalize git URLs to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
222		normalizedURL := m.normalizeGitURL(originURL)
223	
224		return m.createHash(normalizedURL), nil
225	}
226	
227	// normalizeGitURL normalizes a git URL to ensure HTTPS and SSH URLs for the same repo generate the same ID
228	// It extracts the host and repository path, normalizing different URL formats to a common form
229	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) normalizeGitURL(gitURL string) string {
230		// Remove .git suffix if present
231		normalized := strings.TrimSuffix(gitURL, ".git")
232	
233		// Handle SSH format (git@host:owner/repo or user@host:owner/repo)
234		if strings.Contains(normalized, "@") && strings.Contains(normalized, ":") {
235			// Split by @ to get the host part
236			parts := strings.SplitN(normalized, "@", 2)
237			if len(parts) == 2 {
238				// Split by : to separate host from path
239				hostAndPath := strings.SplitN(parts[1], ":", 2)
240				if len(hostAndPath) == 2 {
241					// Return normalized as "host/path"
242					return hostAndPath[0] + "/" + hostAndPath[1]
243				}
244			}
245		}
246	
247		// Handle HTTPS and other protocol URLs (https://host/owner/repo, git://host/owner/repo, etc.)
248		if strings.Contains(normalized, "://") {
249			// Remove the protocol
250			protocolIndex := strings.Index(normalized, "://")
251			if protocolIndex != -1 {
252				// Return everything after the protocol
253				return normalized[protocolIndex+3:]
254			}
255		}
256	
257		// Handle implicit format (host/owner/repo) - already normalized
258		return normalized
259	}
260	
261	// createHash creates a hash from the input string matching the TypeScript implementation
262	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) createHash(input string) string {
263		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(input))
264		fullHash := fmt.Sprintf("%x", hash)
265	
266		// Take first 16 characters and format as UUID-like string
267		truncated := fullHash[:16]
268	
269		// Insert dashes after every 4 characters: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
270		formatted := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s-%s-%s",
271			truncated[0:4],
272			truncated[4:8],
273			truncated[8:12],
274			truncated[12:16])
275	
276		return formatted
277	}
278	
279	// GetWorkspaceID returns the workspace ID from the project identity
280	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetWorkspaceID() (string, error) {
281		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
282		if err != nil {
283			return "", err
284		}
285		if identity == nil || identity.WorkspaceID == "" {
286			return "", fmt.Errorf("no workspace ID found")
287		}
288		return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
289	}
290	
291	// GetGitID returns the git ID from the project identity
292	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetGitID() (string, error) {
293		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
294		if err != nil {
295			return "", err
296		}
297		if identity == nil || identity.GitID == "" {
298			return "", fmt.Errorf("no git ID found")
299		}
300		return identity.GitID, nil
301	}
302	
303	// GetProjectID returns the git ID if available, otherwise the workspace ID
304	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetProjectID() (string, error) {
305		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
306		if err != nil {
307			return "", err
308		}
309		if identity == nil {
310			return "", fmt.Errorf("no project identity found")
311		}
312	
313		// Prefer git_id over workspace_id (like the TypeScript implementation)
314		if identity.GitID != "" {
315			return identity.GitID, nil
316		}
317		if identity.WorkspaceID != "" {
318			return identity.WorkspaceID, nil
319		}
320	
321		return "", fmt.Errorf("no project ID available")
322	}
323	
324	// ValidateUUID checks if a string is a valid UUID format
325	func ValidateUUID(s string) bool {
326		_, err := uuid.Parse(s)
327		return err == nil
328	}
329	
330	// IsValidProjectID checks if a string matches the expected project ID format
331	// Project IDs are 16 hex characters formatted as: xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx
332	func IsValidProjectID(s string) bool {
333		// Match the format: 4 groups of 4 hex characters separated by dashes
334		matched, _ := regexp.MatchString(`^[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}$`, s)
335		return matched
336	}
337	
338	// parseGitRemoteURL parses a git remote URL and returns the repository name
339	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) parseGitRemoteURL(remoteURL string) string {
340		// Remove .git suffix if present
341		withoutDotGit := strings.TrimSuffix(remoteURL, ".git")
342	
343		var repoName string
344	
345		// Case 1: SSH format (git@github.com:owner/repo)
346		if strings.HasPrefix(withoutDotGit, "git@") {
347			// Split by : and get the part after it
348			parts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, ":")
349			if len(parts) >= 2 {
350				pathParts := strings.Split(parts[1], "/")
351				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
352					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
353				}
354			}
355		} else if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^(https?|git|git\+ssh)://`).MatchString(withoutDotGit); matched {
356			// Case 2: HTTP-like protocols (http://, https://, git://, git+ssh://)
357			// Remove the protocol part and extract repo name
358			urlParts := strings.Split(withoutDotGit, "/")
359			if len(urlParts) > 0 {
360				repoName = urlParts[len(urlParts)-1]
361			}
362		} else {
363			// Case 3: Implicit HTTPS (github.com/owner/repo)
364			// Match domain/path pattern
365			if matched := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+/(.+)`).FindStringSubmatch(withoutDotGit); len(matched) > 1 {
366				pathParts := strings.Split(matched[1], "/")
367				if len(pathParts) > 0 {
368					repoName = pathParts[len(pathParts)-1]
369				}
370			}
371		}
372	
373		return repoName
374	}
375	
376	// generateProjectName generates a project name from git remote or directory name
377	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) generateProjectName() string {
378		// First, try to get from git remote
379		gitConfigPath := filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, ".git", "config")
380	
381		if _, err := os.Stat(gitConfigPath); err == nil {
382			// Git config exists, try to parse it
383			gitConfig, err := os.ReadFile(gitConfigPath)
384			if err == nil {
385				// Find origin remote URL using regex
386				originURLRegex := regexp.MustCompile(`\[remote "origin"\][^\[]*url\s*=\s*([^\n\r]+)`)
387				matches := originURLRegex.FindSubmatch(gitConfig)
388				if len(matches) >= 2 {
389					originURL := strings.TrimSpace(string(matches[1]))
390					if repoName := m.parseGitRemoteURL(originURL); repoName != "" {
391						return repoName
392					}
393				}
394			}
395		}
396	
397		// Fallback: use the last component of the project directory
398		return filepath.Base(m.projectRoot)
399	}
400	
401	// GetProjectName returns the project name from the project identity
402	func (m *ProjectIdentityManager) GetProjectName() (string, error) {
403		identity, err := m.ReadProjectIdentity()
404		if err != nil {
405			return "", err
406		}
407		if identity == nil || identity.ProjectName == "" {
408			return "", fmt.Errorf("no project name found")
409		}
410		return identity.ProjectName, nil
411	}
412
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:21Z)**_

Confirmed — this is native to the CLI, not just something lore reads. `pkg/utils/project_identity.go` owns it, and `pkg/cloud/sync.go` + `main.go` consume it. So lore is a *downstream consumer* of an identity the CLI already mints; I had the dependency arrow backwards in my last message. Worth being precise about what it actually gives us:

**What `git_id` is:** `sha256(normalized origin URL)` → first 16 hex as `xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx`. The normalization strips protocol, `@`, `:`, and `.git` so `git@github.com:org/repo.git` and `https://github.com/org/repo` collapse to the same `github.com/org/repo` → same hash. That's exactly why it's stable across dir / machine / user: it's derived from the *remote*, nothing local. `GetProjectID()` returns `git_id` when present, else falls back to `workspace_id`.

**The one caveat that matters for our staging:** `workspace_id` is `sha256(abs path)` — machine-local. So the "same project across machines/users" guarantee holds **only when there's a git origin**. A local-only repo (no remote) has *just* `workspace_id`, which is a different value on every machine and in every clone dir. That maps cleanly onto the portability doc's stages:

- **Stage 1 (cross-provider, same dir)** — identity is irrelevant; same `project_id` either way.
- **Stage 2 (cross-project, local)** — `git_id` is precisely what lets the picker *label and group* sessions by origin project, and lets us recognize "this session came from project A" when resuming it into project B's directory. Note cross-project resume is about moving *between* two different `git_id`s, so identity here is for labeling/filtering the picker, not for matching.
- **Stages 3–4 (cross-machine / cross-user)** — `git_id` is the join key that makes "same project, different machine/user" even expressible. Anything remote-less can't cross that boundary by identity alone, which is a real product constraint to surface, not paper over.

So for the restore index, the takeaway is concrete: **`project_id` (git_id-preferred) is the project column**, and every session row carries it — same key lore uses, same key cloud sync uses, so the index, the cloud, and lore all speak one identity. The session-level key is then `project_id + session uuid`.

That raises one design fork for the index that your `.project.json` point actually sharpens. Want me to lay out where the restore index should *live and key itself*?

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:22Z)**_

**Where should the restore index live and how should it be keyed?**

Options:
- **One global index** - A single ~/.specstory/restore.db (or similar) indexing every project's sessions, each row tagged with project_id. One place to search across all projects — natural fit for cross-project resume since the whole point is seeing sessions from other projects. Mirrors lore's single-corpus model.
- **Per-project index** - A .specstory/restore.db inside each project, indexing only that project's sessions. Local, travels with the repo, but cross-project resume must discover and union many DBs at query time.
- **You decide / discuss** - Lay out the full trade-off (discovery cost, cloud-sync alignment, lore coexistence) and recommend, rather than me picking now.


**Answer:** You decide / discuss

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:23Z)**_

Here's the full trade-off, then my recommendation.

## The core tension

Cross-project resume's defining UX requirement is *"show me sessions from other projects, from one place."* That single sentence is what discriminates the two layouts:

| Dimension | Global index (`~/.specstory/restore.db`) | Per-project index (`<proj>/.specstory/restore.db`) |
|---|---|---|
| **Cross-project pick** | one query, naturally spans all projects | must discover every `.specstory` on disk + open/union N DBs at pick time |
| **Discovery cost** | scan once at index time, amortized | every `restore` invocation pays a full-disk `scanForHistories` walk before it can show anything |
| **Travels with repo** | no — machine-local cache | yes — but that's a *liability* here (see below) |
| **Cloud alignment (Stage 3/4)** | high — it's the local mirror of "all sessions I can access," keyed exactly like cloud | low — cloud is inherently cross-project; per-project DBs fight that grain |
| **Lore coexistence** | sits beside `~/.specstory/lore.db`, same dir, same identity, can share the discovery/identity code | divergent — lore is global, restore would be scattered |
| **Staleness / ownership** | one writer, one rebuild path | N files to keep fresh, each lagging independently |

## Why "travels with the repo" is a trap here

The per-project layout's one real advantage — the index lives inside the repo — is actively wrong for this feature. A `restore.db` inside `.specstory/` would either get committed (leaking *other sessions'* content and FTS into the repo, a privacy problem) or need gitignoring (so it doesn't travel anyway, killing the only advantage). Either way it's a machine-local cache pretending to be project data. The `.specstory/history/*.md` files are the per-project source of truth; the index over them should not also be per-project.

## My recommendation: one global, rebuildable index

`~/.specstory/restore.db`, every row tagged `project_id` (git_id-preferred, `workspace_id` fallback — reusing `GetProjectID()` verbatim), session key `project_id + uuid`. Critically, treat it as a **derived cache, not a source of truth**: the authoritative data stays the native session stores and the rendered `.specstory/history` markdown. That means the global file can be deleted and rebuilt by scanning, so we get global-query convenience without making the file precious — and it inherits lore's exact idempotency discipline (`size + mtime + parser_version` skip, prune for vanished files).

This aligns on all the axes that matter: it's the grain the cross-project picker wants, it's the grain the cloud will eventually serve (Stage 3/4 just swaps the *source* feeding the same-shaped local index), and it lives next to `lore.db` sharing one identity vocabulary.

## Two sub-decisions this opens (don't need answers now, just flagging)

1. **One DB or two, beside lore?** I lean **two separate DBs** (`lore.db` stays signal-extraction-from-markdown with its own `PARSER_VERSION`; `restore.db` is session-metadata + FTS + maybe `SessionData`, fed by the Go providers from raw JSONL). Different lifecycles and different inputs — coupling them couples two parser-version cadences. They can still share the discovery + identity libs since both are now in-process Go.

2. **Does the index store `SessionData`, or just metadata + FTS?** For Stages 1–2 (local), no — we re-parse on demand through the existing forward path, exactly as the doc says, so the index only needs *metadata to drive the picker* + *FTS to find a session*. Storing serialized `SessionData` only becomes load-bearing for Stages 3–4, where the cloud is the source. I'd defer the blob until then to avoid carrying a heavy, version-fragile payload before it earns its place.

Want me to turn this into a concrete schema sketch for `restore.db` (the picker columns + the FTS5 content table + how it's fed from the providers), or first nail down decision #1/#2 with you?

```text
package spi

import (
	"context"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
)

// CheckResult contains the result of a provider check operation
type CheckResult struct {
	Success      bool   // Whether the check succeeded
	Version      string // Version of the provider (empty on failure)
	Location     string // File path/location of the provider executable
	ErrorMessage string // Error message if check failed (empty on success)
}

// ProgressCallback reports progress during session parsing/processing
// current: number of items processed so far (1-based)
// total: total number of items to process
type ProgressCallback func(current, total int)

// AgentChatSession represents a chat session from an AI coding agent
type AgentChatSession struct {
	SessionID   string              // Stable and unique identifier for the session (often a UUID)
	CreatedAt   string              // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
	Slug        string              // Stable human-readable but file name safe slug, often derived from first user message
	SessionData *schema.SessionData // Structured session data in unified format
	RawData     string              // Raw session data (e.g., JSON blobs for Cursor CLI, JSONL for Claude Code and Codex CLI, etc.)
}

// SessionMetadata contains lightweight metadata about a session without full content
// Used by ListAgentChatSessions for efficient session listing
type SessionMetadata struct {
	SessionID string `json:"session_id" csv:"session_id"` // Stable and unique identifier for the session
	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at" csv:"created_at"` // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
	Slug      string `json:"slug" csv:"slug"`             // Stable human-readable session name/slug
	Name      string `json:"name" csv:"name"`             // Human-readable description of the session (may be empty if not available)
}

// Provider defines the interface that all agent coding tool providers must implement
type Provider interface {
	// Name returns the human-readable name of the provider (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor CLI", "Codex CLI")
	Name() string

	// Check verifies if the provider is properly installed and returns version info
	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary path, non-empty = use this specific command/path
	Check(customCommand string) CheckResult

	// DetectAgent checks if the provider's agent has been used in the given project path
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// helpOutput: if true AND no activity is found, the provider should output helpful guidance
	// Returns true if the agent has created artifacts/sessions in the specified path
	DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool

	// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a single chat session by ID for the given project path
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// sessionID: specific session identifier to retrieve (always provided, never empty)
	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
	// Returns nil if the session is not found, error for actual errors
	GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*AgentChatSession, error)

	// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves all chat sessions for the given project path
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
	// progress: optional callback for reporting progress during parsing (nil = no progress reporting)
	// Returns a slice of AgentChatSession structs containing session data
	GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress ProgressCallback) ([]AgentChatSession, error)

	// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight metadata for all sessions without full parsing
	// This should be faster than GetAgentChatSessions as it only needs to return SessionMetadata and not the full AgentChatSession
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// Returns a slice of SessionMetadata (ordering is provider-defined; consumers will sort if needed)
	ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]SessionMetadata, error)

	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the agent in interactive mode and watches for session updates
	// Blocks until the agent exits, calling sessionCallback for each new/updated session
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// customCommand: empty string = use detected/default binary with default args, non-empty = use this specific command/path
	// resumeSessionID: empty string = start new session, non-empty = resume this specific session ID
	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
	// sessionCallback is called with each session update (provider should not block on callback)
	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
	ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error

	// WatchAgent watches for agent activity and calls the callback with an updated AgentChatSession
	// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for new activity
	// Runs until error or context cancellation
	// ctx: Context for cancellation and timeout control
	// projectPath: Agent's working directory
	// debugRaw: if true, provider should write provider-specific raw debug files to .specstory/debug/<sessionID>/
	//           (e.g., numbered JSON files). The unified session-data.json is written centrally by the CLI.
	// sessionCallback: called with AgentChatSession on each update (provider should not block on callback)
	// The implementation should handle its own file watching and session tracking
	WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*AgentChatSession)) error

	// ReconstructSession rebuilds the provider's native session format from the
	// neutral SessionData so the agent can resume the conversation (the reverse of
	// the parse/generate path). It is a pure transform: it returns the native file
	// bytes, a freshly minted native-format session ID, and a suggested filename,
	// but does NOT touch the filesystem — writing into the live agent store is the
	// caller's responsibility.
	//
	// Every provider carries this responsibility; providers that do not yet have a
	// native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
	// See docs/SESSION-PORTABILITY.md for the design.
	ReconstructSession(data *schema.SessionData, opts ReconstructOptions) (*ReconstructedSession, error)

	// NativeSessionPath resolves the absolute path where a reconstructed session
	// file (with the given base filename, from ReconstructedSession.Filename)
	// belongs in this provider's native store for the given project, WITHOUT
	// requiring the directory to exist — the caller creates it and writes the file.
	// Providers without a native serializer return ErrReconstructionUnsupported.
	NativeSessionPath(projectPath string, filename string) (string, error)
}
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:27Z)**_

This is the right question to be chewing on, and grounding it in the SPI surface sharpens it. The whole interface is `projectPath`-scoped today — every `List/Get/WatchAgentChatSessions` takes a project dir and maps it *forward* to a native store location. What you're describing inverts that, and the inversion has consequences worth pulling out.

## Frame restore.db as a derived cache — that decides most of the WHEN

If restore.db is a cache over an authoritative source (the native stores), then the two cases aren't really "two ways things get in" — they're **warm** vs **cold**, and the rule that falls out is: *catchup must always be able to fully rebuild it from scratch.* Incremental upsert is purely an optimization to keep it warm. The danger to avoid is letting incremental be the *only* path, because then any gap becomes permanent. So:

- **Steady state** — yes, upsert at every sighting: `sync`, `run`, `watch`, and `resume`. The nice property here is these hooks already hand you a parsed `SessionData`, so they can populate *both* the metadata row *and* the FTS content in one shot, for free. `resume` is a double-write: re-index the source session, and index the freshly reconstructed target session so it's itself resumable next time.
- **Catchup** — the cold rebuild: db missing / blown away / explicit `--reindex`. This is where the new capability lives.

## The gap you flagged can't be closed by adding more hooks

Sessions specstory never saw won't be in the db — true — but the fix is *not* more event points. There will always be an agent session run without specstory attached. Completeness through event-hook coverage is a losing chase. The honest answer is: **the catchup scan is the gap-closer**, run opportunistically (e.g. on `resume` when the db looks stale) rather than trying to witness every session live. That reframes the gap from "a hole we must plug" to "a thing the periodic scan sweeps up."

## The new SPI capability is project-*discovering*, not project-*scoped* — that's the real shift

Today: `projectPath → store location → sessions`. What you want: `enumerate every native session → discover which project each belongs to`. The project becomes an **output**, not an input. That changes the return shape: a global-list method has to surface each session's **originating cwd**, because that's the only thing that lets us attribute it to a project.

Codex proves the point: its sessions aren't in a project-keyed directory at all — they're under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/`, and the cwd lives *inside* the rollout file. Claude's store dir *is* the cwd-encoded path, so it's recoverable there too, but uniformly the right contract is "provider returns sessions + their cwd; the CLI resolves cwd → `project_id`." Keep identity resolution in the CLI's existing `ProjectIdentityManager` (single source of truth), not duplicated per provider.

So I'd shape it as something like a `ListAllAgentChatSessions()` returning lightweight refs (session id, created, slug, **native path**, **origin cwd**) — metadata-cheap, no full parse. Don't have it return `SessionData`.

## Monorepo: `git_id` already dissolves most of it — and that argues the global primitive is also how you answer "this project"

Because `git_id` = hash of the *origin remote*, any cwd anywhere inside the repo boundary — child, peer, parent — resolves to the **same** `git_id` once you walk up to `.git` and read the remote. So the monorepo "launched from different dirs" case is handled automatically *as long as we resolve each session's cwd to its git root*.

The sharp consequence: the cwd-scoped store lookup (Claude's `~/.claude/projects/<this-exact-cwd>/`) is actually **too narrow even for the current project** — a session launched from a sibling dir of the same repo sits under a *different* encoded dir and the project-scoped list misses it. So "all sessions for THIS project" in a monorepo is best answered by *enumerate-all-then-filter-by-`git_id`*, the very same global primitive — not by the forward store lookup. That means the new capability isn't only for catchup; it's the correct primitive for the monorepo current-project view too. (The one case that still fragments: remote-less dirs, which only have the path-based `workspace_id` — flag it, don't pretend it's solved.)

## One layering idea that makes catchup cheap

Split the db into two fill-levels:

1. **Existence + metadata** (project_id, uuid, agent, date, slug, native path) — populated cheaply from the global enumeration, enough to *browse and pick*.
2. **Content / FTS** — requires parsing `SessionData`, so populate it from the steady-state hooks (where it's free) and **backfill lazily** for cold sessions (e.g. when the user actually searches, or on a background pass).

That way catchup over thousands of sessions is a fast metadata sweep, not a full re-parse, and full-text search fills in over time without ever blocking the picker.

---

My recommendation in one line: **restore.db is a cache; incremental upserts keep it warm; a new project-discovering global-enumeration SPI method is the cold-rebuild + monorepo primitive; metadata fills cheaply and FTS fills opportunistically.**

Want me to spec the exact shape/signature of that global-enumeration SPI method (and how each existing provider would implement it against its native store layout) — still as design, no code?

---

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:30Z)**_


```md
# Restore Index (`restore.db`)

> **STATUS: STRAWMAN — NOT RATIFIED.** This document captures an in-progress design
> conversation about how `specstory restore` should know which sessions exist. Nothing
> here is agreed. It is written down so we can argue with a concrete target. Sections
> marked **OPEN** are unresolved; treat the rest as a proposal, not a decision.

## Purpose

`specstory restore` (cross-provider and, next, cross-project resume — see
[SESSION-PORTABILITY.md](SESSION-PORTABILITY.md)) needs a good selection UX: *"show me the
session I want to continue, wherever it came from."* For cross-project resume in
particular, that means surfacing sessions that originated in **other** projects, from one
place, with full-text search to find them.

Today the SPI answers only a narrower question — *"for this project dir, what sessions
exist?"* — by mapping a `projectPath` forward to a native store location. The restore
experience needs a broader, searchable view across everything we know about. `restore.db`
is the proposed index that backs it.

This doc is about **where that index lives, and WHEN entries get into it** — not the
column-level schema (that is deliberately deferred; see [Non-Goals](#non-goals)).

## Decisions taken so far

These two were settled in conversation:

- **One global index, not per-project.** A single machine-level `restore.db` (proposed
  `~/.specstory/restore.db`) indexes sessions from every project, each row tagged with
  `project_id`. Cross-project resume's defining need is to see across projects from one
  place; a per-project index would force a full-disk discovery + N-database union at every
  pick, and a per-project index file inside `.specstory/` is either a privacy leak (if
  committed) or doesn't travel (if gitignored) — so it earns nothing.
- **Separate from `lore.db`.** Lore keeps its own corpus (`~/.specstory/lore.db`), with its
  own `PARSER_VERSION` cadence, fed by parsing rendered markdown. `restore.db` is a new,
  independent database fed by the Go providers from raw native session data. Different
  inputs, different lifecycles — coupling them would couple two unrelated version cadences.
  They can still share discovery and the project-identity code.

## Guiding principle: `restore.db` is a derived cache

The authoritative source of truth is, and stays, the **native session stores** (and the
`.specstory/history` renders). `restore.db` is a **cache** over them. Two consequences drive
the rest of this design:

1. **It must be fully rebuildable from scratch at any time.** Deleting it is always safe.
2. **Incremental population is an optimization, never the only path.** If the only way data
   got in were live event hooks, any session we failed to witness would be missing
   *forever*. Because catchup can always rebuild, gaps are transient, not permanent.

## Project identity is already solved — reuse it

`pkg/utils/project_identity.go` already mints the identity this index needs:

- `git_id` = `sha256(normalized origin remote URL)` → `xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx`. Derived from the
  remote, so it is **stable across directory, machine, and user** — the cross-project /
  cross-machine join key.
- `workspace_id` = `sha256(absolute path)` — machine-local fallback for repos with no remote.
- `GetProjectID()` returns `git_id` when present, else `workspace_id`.

Every `restore.db` row carries `project_id` from `GetProjectID()` — the same identity the
cloud sync and Lore already speak, so the index, the cloud, and Lore share one vocabulary.

**Caveat to keep honest:** the "same project across machines/users" guarantee holds **only
when a git remote exists**. A remote-less repo has only the path-based `workspace_id`, which
differs per machine and per clone directory. This is a real product constraint for the later
cross-machine/cross-user stages, not something this index can paper over.

## When do entries get into `restore.db`?

Two modes: **steady state** (warm, incremental) and **catchup** (cold, full rebuild).

### Steady state — incremental upsert at every sighting

The commands that already parse sessions upsert into `restore.db` as a side effect. They
already hold a parsed `SessionData`, so they can populate the metadata row **and** the
full-text content in one shot, for free:

- `specstory sync` — every session it processes.
- `specstory run` / `specstory watch` — every session update the watcher sees.
- `specstory restore` — a **double write**: re-index the source session, and index the
  freshly reconstructed target session so it is itself resumable next time.

### The witness gap — and why more hooks don't close it

Event-driven population only sees sessions specstory was attached to. An agent session run
without specstory will be absent. **This gap cannot be closed by adding more event points** —
there is always a session we didn't witness. The gap-closer is the catchup scan (below), run
opportunistically (e.g. when `restore` finds the index stale or empty), not an ever-growing
list of hooks. The reframe: the gap is something the periodic scan *sweeps up*, not a hole we
must plug live.

### Catchup — cold rebuild / explicit reindex

Triggered when `restore.db` is missing, was blown away, is detected stale, or the user
explicitly asks (proposed `specstory restore --reindex` / a `catchup` verb — **OPEN**, naming
not decided). This is the path that enumerates *everything* and is the reason for the new SPI
capability below.

## The missing SPI capability: project-*discovering* enumeration

Today's listing is project-**scoped**: `projectPath → store location → sessions`. Catchup
needs the inverse — enumerate **every** native session, then **discover** which project each
belongs to. The project becomes an *output*, not an input.

That changes the return shape. A global enumeration must surface each session's **originating
cwd**, because the cwd is the only thing that lets the CLI attribute the session to a
`project_id`. Identity resolution stays in the CLI's `ProjectIdentityManager` (one source of
truth) — providers surface the cwd, they do not compute `project_id`.

Proposed shape (**OPEN** — names and exact fields illustrative, not final):

\```go
// ListAllAgentChatSessions enumerates every session in this provider's native store,
// regardless of project. Unlike ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath), the project is not an
// input — each returned ref carries the originating cwd so the caller can resolve project
// identity. Lightweight: no full SessionData parse.
ListAllAgentChatSessions() ([]GlobalSessionRef, error)

type GlobalSessionRef struct {
    SessionID  string // native session id (uuid)
    CreatedAt  string // ISO 8601
    Slug       string // human-readable, filename-safe
    NativePath string // absolute path to the native session file
    OriginCwd  string // working dir the session was launched from (→ project_id)
}
\```

Why each provider's implementation differs (the inversion is not uniform):

- **Claude Code** — store dir *is* the cwd-encoded path (`~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/`),
  so cwd is recoverable from the directory name; enumerate all project dirs.
- **Codex CLI** — sessions live under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/`, **not** project-keyed
  at all. The cwd is *inside* the rollout file (`session_meta` / `turn_context`). Proof that
  "the project is a property discovered from the session," not from its location.
- **Others** (Gemini, Droid, DeepSeek, Cursor) — each has its own layout; the contract is the
  same: return sessions + their cwd.

## Monorepos: `git_id` dissolves most of it

Because `git_id` hashes the *remote*, any cwd inside a repo boundary — child, peer, or parent
directory — resolves to the **same** `git_id` once we walk up to `.git` and read origin. So
sessions launched from different directories of one monorepo attribute to one project
automatically, *provided we resolve each session's cwd to its git root*.

Sharp consequence: the cwd-scoped store lookup is **too narrow even for the current project**
— a session launched from a sibling directory of the same repo lives under a *different*
encoded store dir and a project-scoped list misses it. So "all sessions for THIS project" in a
monorepo is best answered by **enumerate-all-then-filter-by-`git_id`** — the same global
primitive — not by the forward store lookup. The new capability is therefore not only a
catchup tool; it is also the correct primitive for the monorepo current-project view.

Still fragments: remote-less directories (only `workspace_id`, which is path-based). Flagged,
not solved.

## Two fill-levels (keeps catchup cheap)

A full re-parse of thousands of sessions on every cold rebuild would be slow. Proposed split:

1. **Existence + metadata** — `project_id`, `uuid`, `agent`, `date`, `slug`, native path.
   Populated cheaply from the global enumeration (no `SessionData` parse). Enough to *browse
   and pick*.
2. **Content / full-text** — requires parsing `SessionData`. Populated for free from the
   steady-state hooks, and **backfilled lazily** for cold sessions (e.g. when the user
   actually searches, or via a background pass).

This way catchup is a fast metadata sweep, full-text search fills in over time, and the picker
never blocks on parsing.

## Non-Goals (for this doc)

- **Column-level schema.** Tables, indexes, FTS5 configuration — deferred until the WHEN and
  the SPI shape are agreed.
- **Whether `restore.db` stores serialized `SessionData`.** For local Stages 1–2 we re-parse
  on demand, so the index needs only metadata + full-text. A `SessionData` blob becomes
  relevant for cloud Stages 3–4 (where the cloud is the source). Deferred.
- **The picker UX itself.** Menu vs TUI, sort/filter controls — separate from how the index is
  populated.

## Open questions

- **OPEN — Catchup trigger and cadence.** On `resume` when stale? Explicit verb/flag? Opt-in
  background pass? What counts as "stale"?
- **OPEN — SPI method name and signature.** `ListAllAgentChatSessions` + `GlobalSessionRef`
  are placeholders.
- **OPEN — Staleness detection.** Reuse Lore's `size + mtime + parser_version` fingerprint per
  native file? Per-store mtime? Something cheaper?
- **OPEN — Eviction / prune.** When a native session is deleted, when does its row leave
  `restore.db`? (Lore prunes on rescan when the file is gone.)
- **OPEN — Remote-less projects.** How (if at all) cross-project/-machine works for repos with
  only a `workspace_id`.
- **OPEN — Lazy FTS backfill mechanics.** On-search? Background? Bounded?

## Related Documents

- [SESSION-PORTABILITY.md](SESSION-PORTABILITY.md) — the reconstruction / resume design this
  index serves.
- [PROVIDER-SPI.md](PROVIDER-SPI.md) — the provider interface the new enumeration method joins.
- `pkg/utils/project_identity.go` — `git_id` / `workspace_id` / `GetProjectID()`.

```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:37Z)**_

My honest take: **no dot — keep `~/.specstory/restore.db`** — for consistency with its direct sibling.

The contents tell the story:

```
~/.specstory/
├── lore.db          ← data artifact, no dot
├── .project.json    ← (this lives per-project, but the convention: config = dot)
├── .cursorindexingignore
├── cli/  provenance/  ← data dirs, no dot
```

The reasoning:

- **`~/.specstory/` is already hidden**, so a leading dot buys nothing for concealment — it won't hide the file from a normal `ls`, the parent dir already does.
- **The one true analog is `lore.db`** — same kind of thing (a machine-level SQLite data artifact), sitting in the same directory. Naming the two peers differently (`lore.db` vs `.restore.db`) would just look like an accident. The dot-prefixed entries here are *config* (`.project.json`, `.cursorindexingignore`), not data stores.
- **WAL sidecars inherit the name**: SQLite in WAL mode (which lore uses) creates `restore.db-wal` and `restore.db-shm` alongside. `.restore.db-wal` reads slightly oddly; `restore.db-wal` pairs cleanly with `lore.db-wal`.

So I'd match `lore.db` exactly. The doc already says `~/.specstory/restore.db`, so nothing to change unless you want the dot — in which case I'd flip both the convention note and stay consistent. Want me to leave it as-is?

36	func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
37	func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
38	func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }

55	// CreateResumeCommand builds the `specstory resume` command (Stage 1: cross-agent).
56	// It mirrors the run/watch setup, then drives an interactive selection: pick a
57	// source agent + session, pick a target agent, then reconstruct (cross-agent) or
58	// native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
59	func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
60		longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.
61	
62	'resume' lists the sessions found in the current project across all agents, lets you
63	pick one, and lets you choose which installed agent to continue it in. Resuming into a
64	different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.`
65	
66		resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
67			Use:   "resume",
68			Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
69			Long:  longDesc,
70			Args:  cobra.NoArgs,
71			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
72				config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
73				slog.Info("Running in resume mode")
74	
75				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
76	
77				// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
78				debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
79				useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
80				useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
81				outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
82				flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
83				noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
84				onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
85				provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
86				noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")
87	
88				if flagDebugDir != "" {
89					spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
90				}
91	
92				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
93				if err != nil {
94					slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
95					return err
96				}
97	
98				// Interactive selection of source agent, session, and target agent.
99				plan, err := selectResumePlan(registry, cwd, os.Stdin, os.Stdout)
100				if err != nil {
101					return err
102				}
103				if plan == nil {
104					// User quit or nothing to resume; selectResumePlan already explained why.
105					return nil
106				}
107	
108				// Setup output configuration and project identity (needed for auto-save + cloud).
109				outConfig, err := utils.SetupOutputConfig(outputDir, flagDebugDir)
110				if err != nil {
111					return err
112				}
113				if err := utils.EnsureHistoryDirectoryExists(outConfig); err != nil {
114					return err
115				}
116				if _, err := utils.NewProjectIdentityManager(cwd).EnsureProjectIdentity(); err != nil {
117					slog.Error("Failed to ensure project identity", "error", err)
118				}
119	
120				CheckAndWarnAuthentication(noCloudSync)
121				if onlyCloudSync && !cloud.IsAuthenticated() {
122					return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--only-cloud-sync requires authentication. Please run 'specstory login' first"}
123				}
124	
125				analytics.SetAgentProviders([]string{plan.to.Name()})
126				analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventResumeActivated, analytics.Properties{
127					"from_provider": plan.fromID,
128					"to_provider":   plan.toID,
129					"cross_agent":   plan.fromID != plan.toID,
130				})
131	
132				// Resolve the native session ID to resume.
133				resumeSessionID, err := prepareResumeTarget(plan, cwd, os.Stdout)
134				if err != nil {
135					return err
136				}
137	
138				// Context for graceful cancellation (Ctrl+C).
139				ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
140				defer cancel()
141	
142				// Provenance infrastructure (mirrors run/watch).
143				provenanceEngine, provenanceCleanup, err := provenance.StartEngine(provenanceEnabled)
144				if err != nil {
145					return err
146				}
147				defer provenanceCleanup()
148				fsCleanup, err := provenance.StartFSWatcher(ctx, provenanceEngine, cwd)
149				if err != nil {
150					return err
151				}
152				defer fsCleanup()
153	
154				// Auto-save callback: identical behavior to run/watch.
155				sessionCallback := func(sess *spi.AgentChatSession) {
156					if sess == nil {
157						return
158					}
159					_, perr := session.ProcessSingleSession(context.Background(), sess, outConfig, session.ProcessingOptions{
160						OnlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
161						IsAutosave:         true,
162						DebugRaw:           debugRaw,
163						UseUTC:             useUTC,
164						NoTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
165					})
166					if perr != nil {
167						slog.Error("Failed to process session update", "sessionId", sess.SessionID, "error", perr)
168					}
169					provenance.ProcessEvents(ctx, provenanceEngine, sess)
170				}
171	
172				slog.Info("Launching resume", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
173				if err := plan.to.ExecAgentAndWatch(cwd, "", resumeSessionID, debugRaw, sessionCallback); err != nil {
174					slog.Error("Agent resume failed", "provider", plan.to.Name(), "error", err)
175					return err
176				}
177				return nil
178			},
179		}
180	
181		resumeCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
182		resumeCmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
183		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
184		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
185		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
186		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
187		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
188		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
189		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
190		resumeCmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
191		_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
192		resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")
193	
194		return resumeCmd
195	}
196	
197	// prepareResumeTarget produces the native session ID to hand to ExecAgentAndWatch.
198	// Same-agent resume reuses the existing session; cross-agent reconstructs the
199	// source SessionData into the target's native store and returns the fresh ID.
200	func prepareResumeTarget(plan *resumePlan, cwd string, out io.Writer) (string, error) {
201		// Same agent: native resume on the existing session, no transform.
202		if plan.fromID == plan.toID {
203			fprintf(out, "\nResuming %s session %s in place...\n", plan.from.Name(), shortID(plan.sessionID))
204			return plan.sessionID, nil
205		}
206	
207		// Cross-agent: load source SessionData, reconstruct, write into target store.
208		fromSession, err := plan.from.GetAgentChatSession(cwd, plan.sessionID, false)
209		if err != nil {
210			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to load source session: %w", err)
211		}
212		if fromSession == nil || fromSession.SessionData == nil {
213			return "", fmt.Errorf("source session %s has no data to reconstruct", plan.sessionID)
214		}
215	
216		note := fmt.Sprintf("Resumed from a %s session via SpecStory.", plan.from.Name())
217		rec, err := plan.to.ReconstructSession(fromSession.SessionData, spi.ReconstructOptions{
218			WorkspaceRoot: cwd,
219			MigrationNote: note,
220		})
221		if err != nil {
222			if errors.Is(err, spi.ErrReconstructionUnsupported) {
223				return "", utils.ValidationError{Message: fmt.Sprintf(
224					"%s can't yet be a cross-agent resume target. Choose Claude Code or Codex CLI (or resume in %s itself).",
225					plan.to.Name(), plan.from.Name())}
226			}
227			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to reconstruct session for %s: %w", plan.to.Name(), err)
228		}
229	
230		path, err := plan.to.NativeSessionPath(cwd, rec.Filename)
231		if err != nil {
232			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve target session path: %w", err)
233		}
234		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
235			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create target session directory: %w", err)
236		}
237		if err := os.WriteFile(path, rec.Content, 0o644); err != nil {
238			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write reconstructed session: %w", err)
239		}
240	
241		slog.Info("resume: wrote reconstructed session", "path", path, "newID", rec.SessionID)
242		fprintf(out, "\nReconstructed %s session into %s as %s.\n", plan.from.Name(), plan.to.Name(), shortID(rec.SessionID))
243		return rec.SessionID, nil
244	}
245	
246	// selectResumePlan runs the interactive selection. Returns nil (no error) when
247	// there is nothing to resume or the user quits.
248	func selectResumePlan(registry *factory.Registry, projectPath string, in io.Reader, out io.Writer) (*resumePlan, error) {
249		reader := bufio.NewReader(in)
250	
251		// 1. Source agents: those with at least one session in this project.
252		var sources []agentChoice
253		sessionsByID := map[string][]spi.SessionMetadata{}
254		for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
255			provider, err := registry.Get(id)
256			if err != nil {
257				continue
258			}
259			metas, err := provider.ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath)
260			if err != nil {
261				slog.Debug("resume: listing sessions failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
262				continue
263			}
264			if len(metas) == 0 {
265				continue
266			}
267			sortSessionsDesc(metas)
268			sources = append(sources, agentChoice{id: id, provider: provider})
269			sessionsByID[id] = metas
270		}
271		if len(sources) == 0 {
272			fprintln(out, "\nNo resumable sessions found for any agent in this project.")
273			return nil, nil
274		}
275	
276		fprintln(out, "\nResume from which agent?")
277		fprintln(out)
278		for i, s := range sources {
279			metas := sessionsByID[s.id]
280			fprintf(out, "  %d. %s — %d session%s (%s)\n", i+1, s.provider.Name(), len(metas), plural(len(metas)), dateRange(metas))
281		}
282		fromIdx, err := promptIndex(reader, out, "\nEnter number (or q to quit): ", len(sources))
283		if err != nil || fromIdx < 0 {
284			return nil, err
285		}
286		from := sources[fromIdx]
287	
288		// 2. Session within the chosen agent (paginated, reverse-chronological).
289		session := pickSession(reader, out, sessionsByID[from.id])
290		if session == nil {
291			return nil, nil
292		}
293	
294		// 3. Target agent: installed agents (including the source).
295		var targets []agentChoice
296		for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
297			provider, err := registry.Get(id)
298			if err != nil {
299				continue
300			}
301			if provider.Check("").Success {
302				targets = append(targets, agentChoice{id: id, provider: provider})
303			}
304		}
305		if len(targets) == 0 {
306			fprintln(out, "\nNo installed agents found to resume into.")
307			return nil, nil
308		}
309	
310		fprintln(out, "\nResume into which agent?")
311		fprintln(out)
312		for i, t := range targets {
313			label := t.provider.Name()
314			if t.id == from.id {
315				label += " (same agent — native resume)"
316			}
317			fprintf(out, "  %d. %s\n", i+1, label)
318		}
319		toIdx, err := promptIndex(reader, out, "\nEnter number (or q to quit): ", len(targets))
320		if err != nil || toIdx < 0 {
321			return nil, err
322		}
323		to := targets[toIdx]
324	
325		return &resumePlan{
326			from:      from.provider,
327			fromID:    from.id,
328			sessionID: session.SessionID,
329			to:        to.provider,
330			toID:      to.id,
331		}, nil
332	}
333	
334	// pickSession shows a paginated, reverse-chronological list and returns the
335	// chosen session, or nil if the user quits.
336	func pickSession(reader *bufio.Reader, out io.Writer, sessions []spi.SessionMetadata) *spi.SessionMetadata {
337		total := len(sessions)
338		pages := (total + resumePageSize - 1) / resumePageSize
339		page := 0
340	
341		for {
342			start := page * resumePageSize
343			end := min(start+resumePageSize, total)
344	
345			fprintf(out, "\nSessions (page %d of %d):\n\n", page+1, pages)
346			for i := start; i < end; i++ {
347				s := sessions[i]
348				fprintf(out, "  %d. %s  %s\n", i+1, sessionDate(s.CreatedAt), sessionLabel(s))
349			}
350	
351			hint := "Enter a number to pick"
352			if page < pages-1 {
353				hint += ", n=next"
354			}
355			if page > 0 {
356				hint += ", p=prev"
357			}
358			hint += ", q=quit: "
359			fprint(out, "\n"+hint)
360	
361			line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
362			choice := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
363			switch choice {
364			case "n":
365				if page < pages-1 {
366					page++
367				}
368				continue
369			case "p":
370				if page > 0 {
371					page--
372				}
373				continue
374			case "q", "":
375				return nil
376			}
377			// io.EOF with no input also lands here; treat a parse failure as a re-prompt.
378			n, perr := strconv.Atoi(choice)
379			if perr != nil || n < 1 || n > total {
380				fprintln(out, "Invalid choice.")
381				if err != nil {
382					// No more input (e.g., piped/closed stdin); avoid looping forever.
383					return nil
384				}
385				continue
386			}
387			return &sessions[n-1]
388		}
389	}
390	
391	// promptIndex prompts for a 1-based selection and returns a 0-based index, or -1
392	// if the user quits.
393	func promptIndex(reader *bufio.Reader, out io.Writer, prompt string, n int) (int, error) {
394		for {
395			fprint(out, prompt)
396			line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
397			choice := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
398			if choice == "q" || choice == "" {
399				return -1, nil
400			}
401			idx, perr := strconv.Atoi(choice)
402			if perr == nil && idx >= 1 && idx <= n {
403				return idx - 1, nil
404			}
405			fprintln(out, "Invalid choice.")
406			if err != nil {
407				// No more input; stop rather than loop forever.
408				return -1, nil
409			}
410		}
411	}
412	
413	// sortSessionsDesc orders sessions newest-first by CreatedAt (ISO 8601 sorts
414	// lexicographically), falling back to SessionID for stability.
415	func sortSessionsDesc(sessions []spi.SessionMetadata) {
416		sort.SliceStable(sessions, func(i, j int) bool {
417			if sessions[i].CreatedAt != sessions[j].CreatedAt {
418				return sessions[i].CreatedAt > sessions[j].CreatedAt
419			}
420			return sessions[i].SessionID > sessions[j].SessionID
421		})
422	}
423	
424	// dateRange returns a human-readable span of session creation dates.
425	func dateRange(sessions []spi.SessionMetadata) string {
426		var earliest, latest time.Time
427		for _, s := range sessions {
428			t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s.CreatedAt)
429			if err != nil {
430				continue
431			}
432			if earliest.IsZero() || t.Before(earliest) {
433				earliest = t
434			}
435			if t.After(latest) {
436				latest = t
437			}
438		}
439		if earliest.IsZero() {
440			return "dates unknown"
441		}
442		if earliest.Format("2006-01-02") == latest.Format("2006-01-02") {
443			return earliest.Format("Jan 2, 2006")
444		}
445		return earliest.Format("Jan 2, 2006") + " – " + latest.Format("Jan 2, 2006")
446	}
447	
448	// sessionDate formats a session's creation timestamp for the picker.
449	func sessionDate(createdAt string) string {
450		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, createdAt)
451		if err != nil {
452			return createdAt
453		}
454		return t.Local().Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
455	}
456	
457	// sessionLabel returns a human-friendly label for a session, preferring its name,
458	// then slug, then a shortened ID.
459	func sessionLabel(s spi.SessionMetadata) string {
460		switch {
461		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
462			return s.Name
463		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
464			return s.Slug
465		default:
466			return shortID(s.SessionID)
467		}
468	}
469	
470	// shortID abbreviates a session ID for display.
471	func shortID(id string) string {
472		if len(id) <= 13 {
473			return id
474		}
475		return id[:5] + "..." + id[len(id)-5:]
476	}
477	
478	// plural returns "s" unless n == 1.
479	func plural(n int) string {
480		if n == 1 {
481			return ""
482		}
483		return "s"
484	}
485
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:50Z)**_

 `./pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

```text
1	// Package factory provides the provider registry and factory implementation.
2	// This package imports all concrete provider implementations and manages
3	// their registration, avoiding circular dependencies in the SPI layer.
4	package factory
5	
6	import (
7		"fmt"
8		"log/slog"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12	
13		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode"
14		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/codexcli"
15		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli"
16		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseektui"
17		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/droidcli"
18		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli"
19		"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
20	)
21	
22	// Registry manages all registered providers
23	type Registry struct {
24		providers         map[string]spi.Provider // key is the provider ID (e.g., "claude", "cursor")
25		mu                sync.RWMutex
26		initialized       bool
27		providerListCache string    // Cached formatted provider list string
28		providerListOnce  sync.Once // Ensures provider list is built only once
29	}
30	
31	// Global registry instance
32	var (
33		registry = &Registry{
34			providers: make(map[string]spi.Provider),
35		}
36		once sync.Once
37	)
38	
39	// ensureInitialized makes sure providers are registered
40	func (r *Registry) ensureInitialized() {
41		once.Do(func() {
42			r.registerAll()
43		})
44	}
45	
46	// registerAll registers all known providers.
47	// This is the ONLY place that needs to be updated when adding new providers.
48	func (r *Registry) registerAll() {
49		r.mu.Lock()
50		defer r.mu.Unlock()
51	
52		if r.initialized {
53			slog.Debug("Provider registry already initialized, skipping registration")
54			return
55		}
56	
57		slog.Debug("Initializing provider registry")
58	
59		// Register providers with simple IDs
60		// Each provider directly implements the spi.Provider interface
61		claudeProvider := claudecode.NewProvider()
62		r.providers["claude"] = claudeProvider
63		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "claude", "name", claudeProvider.Name())
64	
65		cursorProvider := cursorcli.NewProvider()
66		r.providers["cursor"] = cursorProvider
67		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "cursor", "name", cursorProvider.Name())
68	
69		codexProvider := codexcli.NewProvider()
70		r.providers["codex"] = codexProvider
71		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "codex", "name", codexProvider.Name())
72	
73		geminiProvider := geminicli.NewProvider()
74		r.providers["gemini"] = geminiProvider
75		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "gemini", "name", geminiProvider.Name())
76	
77		droidProvider := droidcli.NewProvider()
78		r.providers["droid"] = droidProvider
79		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "droid", "name", droidProvider.Name())
80	
81		deepseekProvider := deepseektui.NewProvider()
82		r.providers["deepseek"] = deepseekProvider
83		slog.Debug("Registered provider", "id", "deepseek", "name", deepseekProvider.Name())
84	
85		r.initialized = true
86		slog.Info("Provider registry initialized", "count", len(r.providers), "providers", r.ListIDsUnsafe())
87	}
88	
89	// ListIDsUnsafe returns provider IDs without locking (for internal use only)
90	func (r *Registry) ListIDsUnsafe() []string {
91		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
92		for id := range r.providers {
93			ids = append(ids, id)
94		}
95		sort.Strings(ids)
96		return ids
97	}
98	
99	// GetRegistry returns the global registry instance
100	func GetRegistry() *Registry {
101		registry.ensureInitialized()
102		return registry
103	}
104	
105	// Get retrieves a provider by ID (case-insensitive)
106	func (r *Registry) Get(id string) (spi.Provider, error) {
107		r.ensureInitialized()
108		r.mu.RLock()
109		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
110	
111		// Case-insensitive lookup
112		for provID, provider := range r.providers {
113			if strings.EqualFold(provID, id) {
114				slog.Debug("Retrieved provider", "requested_id", id, "matched_id", provID, "name", provider.Name())
115				return provider, nil
116			}
117		}
118	
119		// Log available providers when lookup fails to aid debugging
120		availableIDs := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
121		for id := range r.providers {
122			availableIDs = append(availableIDs, id)
123		}
124		sort.Strings(availableIDs)
125	
126		slog.Warn("Provider not found",
127			"requested_id", id,
128			"available_providers", availableIDs)
129	
130		return nil, fmt.Errorf("provider '%s' not found", id)
131	}
132	
133	// GetAll returns all registered providers as a map of ID to Provider
134	func (r *Registry) GetAll() map[string]spi.Provider {
135		r.ensureInitialized()
136		r.mu.RLock()
137		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
138	
139		// Return a copy to prevent external modification
140		result := make(map[string]spi.Provider)
141		for id, provider := range r.providers {
142			result[id] = provider
143		}
144	
145		return result
146	}
147	
148	// ListIDs returns all registered provider IDs (sorted)
149	func (r *Registry) ListIDs() []string {
150		r.ensureInitialized()
151		r.mu.RLock()
152		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
153	
154		ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
155		for id := range r.providers {
156			ids = append(ids, id)
157		}
158	
159		sort.Strings(ids)
160		return ids
161	}
162	
163	// GetDefault returns the default provider (Claude)
164	func (r *Registry) GetDefault() (spi.Provider, error) {
165		r.ensureInitialized()
166		slog.Debug("Getting default provider (claude)")
167		return r.Get("claude")
168	}
169	
170	// GetProviderList returns a formatted string listing all providers.
171	// Used for help text and error messages.
172	// The result is cached since providers are static for the lifetime of the process.
173	func (r *Registry) GetProviderList() string {
174		r.ensureInitialized()
175	
176		// Build the provider list only once and cache it
177		r.providerListOnce.Do(func() {
178			r.mu.RLock()
179			defer r.mu.RUnlock()
180	
181			if len(r.providers) == 0 {
182				r.providerListCache = "No providers registered"
183				return
184			}
185	
186			// Get sorted IDs for consistent ordering
187			ids := make([]string, 0, len(r.providers))
188			for id := range r.providers {
189				ids = append(ids, id)
190			}
191			sort.Strings(ids)
192	
193			// Build the formatted list by accessing providers directly (no Get() calls)
194			var parts []string
195			for _, id := range ids {
196				if provider, ok := r.providers[id]; ok {
197					parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", id, provider.Name()))
198				}
199			}
200	
201			r.providerListCache = strings.Join(parts, ", ")
202		})
203	
204		return r.providerListCache
205	}
206
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:53Z)**_

 `./pkg/providers/claudecode/path_utils.go`

```text
1	package claudecode
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log/slog"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"regexp"
9		"strings"
10	)
11	
12	// projectDirNameRegex matches any character that is not alphanumeric or a dash.
13	// Claude Code replaces each such character with a dash to map a working directory
14	// to a project folder name under ~/.claude/projects.
15	var projectDirNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-zA-Z0-9-]`)
16	
17	// encodeProjectDirName converts a real (symlink-resolved) path into Claude Code's
18	// project directory name: non-alphanumeric/dash characters become dashes, with a
19	// guaranteed leading dash. Example: "/Users/sean/app" -> "-Users-sean-app".
20	func encodeProjectDirName(realPath string) string {
21		name := projectDirNameRegex.ReplaceAllString(realPath, "-")
22		if !strings.HasPrefix(name, "-") {
23			name = "-" + name
24		}
25		return name
26	}
27	
28	// resolveClaudeProjectDir returns ~/.claude/projects/<encoded> for the given
29	// project path, resolving symlinks, WITHOUT requiring the directory to exist.
30	// Used when writing a reconstructed session into the store (the caller creates
31	// the directory). Distinct from GetClaudeCodeProjectDir, which requires the
32	// projects directory to already exist.
33	func resolveClaudeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
34		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
35		if err != nil {
36			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
37		}
38	
39		cwd := projectPath
40		if cwd == "" {
41			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
42			if err != nil {
43				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
44			}
45		}
46	
47		// Resolve symlinks to match Claude Code's behavior; fall back to the raw path
48		// if resolution fails (e.g., a path component does not exist yet).
49		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
50		if err != nil {
51			realPath = cwd
52		}
53	
54		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects", encodeProjectDirName(realPath)), nil
55	}
56	
57	// GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir returns the path to the Claude Code projects directory
58	func GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir() (string, error) {
59		// Get user's home directory
60		homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
61		if err != nil {
62			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
63		}
64	
65		// Construct the path to .claude/projects
66		projectsDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "projects")
67	
68		// Check if the directory exists
69		if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); err != nil {
70			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
71				return "", fmt.Errorf("claude projects directory not found at %s", projectsDir)
72			}
73			return "", fmt.Errorf("error checking Claude projects directory: %v", err)
74		}
75	
76		return projectsDir, nil
77	}
78	
79	// GetClaudeCodeProjectDir returns the Claude Code project directory for the given path.
80	// If no path is provided (empty string), it uses the current working directory.
81	// It resolves any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior, which uses the real path
82	// when creating project directories. Claude Code requires a specific naming convention to map
83	// working directories to project folders: each non-alphanumeric character is replaced with a dash
84	// to create a unique, filesystem-safe identifier
85	func GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
86		// Use provided path or get current working directory
87		var cwd string
88		var err error
89		if projectPath == "" {
90			cwd, err = os.Getwd()
91			if err != nil {
92				return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
93			}
94		} else {
95			cwd = projectPath
96		}
97	
98		// Resolve any symlinks in the path to match Claude Code's behavior
99		// Claude Code uses the real path when creating project directories, so
100		// if the user's working directory contains symlinks, we need to resolve
101		// them to find the correct project directory in ~/.claude/projects/
102		realPath, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cwd)
103		if err != nil {
104			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve symlinks in working directory: %v", err)
105		}
106	
107		// Log if the path was changed by symlink resolution
108		if realPath != cwd {
109			slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Resolved symlinks in working directory",
110				"original", cwd,
111				"resolved", realPath)
112		}
113	
114		// Convert path to project directory format (matching Claude Code's behavior).
115		// Example: "/Users/sean/My Projects(1)/app" becomes "-Users-sean-My-Projects-1--app"
116		projectDirName := encodeProjectDirName(realPath)
117	
118		// Log the transformation for debugging path issues
119		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Transformed working directory to project name",
120			"cwd", cwd,
121			"projectDirName", projectDirName)
122	
123		// Get Claude projects directory
124		projectsDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectsDir()
125		if err != nil {
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get Claude projects directory: %v", err)
127		}
128	
129		fullProjectPath := filepath.Join(projectsDir, projectDirName)
130		slog.Debug("GetClaudeCodeProjectDir: Constructed full project path",
131			"projectPath", fullProjectPath)
132	
133		return fullProjectPath, nil
134	}
135
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:54Z)**_

A key correction surfaces already: Claude's `encodeProjectDirName` is **lossy** (every non-alphanumeric char → dash), so the cwd is *not* reliably decodable from the directory name — the reliable cwd lives in the JSONL records' `cwd` field (`agent_session.go:96`). That actually unifies the model. Let me confirm the Codex side and look at the existing SQLite-in-Go pattern.

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:18:55Z)**_

 `./pkg/providers/codexcli/path_utils.go`

```text
1	package codexcli
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"path/filepath"
6		"sort"
7		"strings"
8	)
9	
10	// codexSessionsRoot returns the root directory where Codex stores session files.
11	func codexSessionsRoot(homeDir string) string {
12		return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".codex", "sessions")
13	}
14	
15	// normalizeCodexPath resolves a path to an absolute, cleaned representation suitable for comparisons.
16	func normalizeCodexPath(path string) string {
17		if strings.TrimSpace(path) == "" {
18			return ""
19		}
20	
21		cleaned := filepath.Clean(path)
22	
23		absPath, err := filepath.Abs(cleaned)
24		if err == nil {
25			cleaned = absPath
26		}
27	
28		if resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(cleaned); err == nil {
29			cleaned = resolved
30		}
31	
32		return filepath.Clean(cleaned)
33	}
34	
35	// readDirSortedDesc reads directory entries and sorts them descending by name.
36	func readDirSortedDesc(path string) ([]os.DirEntry, error) {
37		entries, err := os.ReadDir(path)
38		if err != nil {
39			return nil, err
40		}
41	
42		sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool {
43			return entries[i].Name() > entries[j].Name()
44		})
45	
46		return entries, nil
47	}
48
```

_**Agent (2026-06-23 14:19:00Z)**_
